title: CHAPTER 09 —
DISTRIBUTION CHARTS
segments.body_html:
When someone summarizes a distribution with a mean, they are making a specific choice about compression. The mean is a s

Chapter 09 — Distribution Charts — This is not an abstract concern. Consider a hypothetical: ten households with incomes of $30,000, $32,000, $35,000, $36,

beats.text: When someone summarizes a distribution with a mean, they are making a specific choice about compression. The mean is a single number that describes the center of mass of the data. It is sensitive to every value , pull one observation to an extreme and the mean moves. It says nothing about spread, nothing about shape, nothing about whether the data is symmetric or skewed or bimodal. This is not an abstract concern.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: THE HISTOGRAM AND
THE BIN-WIDTH PROBLEM
segments.body_html:
Before the box plot and the violin, there is the histogram. It is the most commonly encountered distribution chart becau

The histogram and the bin-width problem — The histogram's critical design decision is the bin width. The choice is more consequential than most people realize.

beats.text: Before the box plot and the violin, there is the histogram. It is the most commonly encountered distribution chart because it appears in every introductory statistics course. It bins continuous data into discrete intervals and shows the count per bin as a column. The histogram's critical design decision is the bin width. The choice is more consequential than most people realize.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · THE HISTOGRAM AND THE BIN-WIDT

segments.section: WHAT THE BOX PLOT SHOWS AND DO
title: WHAT THE BOX PLOT SHOWS AND
DOESN'T
segments.body_html:
The box plot's five-number summary has a specific design. Tukey's choices were deliberate.

What the box plot shows and doesn't — The box spans Q1 to Q3 — the middle half of the data. This is the interquartile range (IQR). The box's height encodes th

beats.text: The box plot's five-number summary has a specific design. Tukey's choices were deliberate. The box spans Q1 to Q3 , the middle half of the data. This is the interquartile range (IQR). The box's height encodes the spread of the central mass of the distribution. A tall box means the middle fifty percent of values are spread widely. A short box means they are compressed.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · WHAT THE BOX PLOT SHOWS AND DO

title: VIOLIN PLOTS:
SHAPE MADE VISIBLE
segments.body_html:
A violin plot draws a kernel density estimate symmetrically around a vertical axis. The width at any point represents th

Violin plots: shape made visible — Kernel density estimation is the machinery underneath the violin. Each observation in the dataset contributes a small sm

beats.text: A violin plot draws a kernel density estimate symmetrically around a vertical axis. The width at any point represents the density of observations at that value. Kernel density estimation is the machinery underneath the violin. Each observation in the dataset contributes a small smooth function , a kernel, typically Gaussian , centered at that value. Sum the contributions of all observations and you get a continuous density estimate. Where observations cluster densely, the sum is large and the violin is wide.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · VIOLIN PLOTS: SHAPE MADE VISIB

title: CAIRO'S GRAPHICACY
CONSTRAINT.
segments.body_html:
Stem-and-leaf — College-educated
Histogram — General public
Box plot — Statistically trained
Violin plot — Visualization-trained

beats.text: Graphicacy is the capacity to read visual representations of data. Just as literacy varies , some readers handle complex prose, others need simpler text , graphicacy varies. Some readers handle violin plots fluently. Others need histograms with annotations. Others need the numbers in a table. This is not a hierarchy. It is a constraint. A chart that exceeds the audience's graphicacy fails as communication, regardless of how technically informative it is. The distribution chart family spans a wide graphicacy range.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · CAIRO'S GRAPHICACY CONSTRAINT

title: STEM-AND-LEAF AND
DENSITY PLOTS
segments.body_html:
Two less-common forms are worth naming because they fill specific niches neither the histogram nor the box plot nor the

Stem-and-leaf and density plots — A stem-and-leaf plot preserves the original data values while showing the distribution. The "stem" is the leading digit

beats.text: Two less-common forms are worth naming because they fill specific niches neither the histogram nor the box plot nor the violin can fill. A stem-and-leaf plot preserves the original data values while showing the distribution. The "stem" is the leading digit of each value; the "leaf" is the trailing digit. Each observation is represented as its leaf placed next to its stem.

segments.label: 06 / 13 · STEM-AND-LEAF AND DENSITY PLOT

title: THE FORM SELECTION
IN PRACTICE.
segments.body_html: What does the audience need to see?→What is the audience's graphicacy level?→What is the sample size?

beats.text: The choice between distribution charts reduces to three questions. What does the audience need to see? Comparison across groups goes to box plot. Shape including multimodality goes to violin plot or KDE. Raw values preserved goes to stem-and-leaf. Overall shape for a general audience goes to histogram.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · THE FORM SELECTION IN PRACTICE

segments.section: THE DESIGN DECISIONS IN THE PA
title: THE DESIGN DECISIONS IN
THE PANTRY CHART.
segments.body_html:
Return to box-whisker.html. Every decision Tukey's design makes is doing work.

The design decisions in the pantry chart — The sort by median positions the best-performing domain on the left, giving the reader an immediate ranking alongside th

beats.text: Return to box-whisker.html. Every decision Tukey's design makes is doing work. The sort by median positions the best-performing domain on the left, giving the reader an immediate ranking alongside the distribution summary. The shared y-axis lets the reader compare distributions without rescaling , a box at the same height in two panels represents the same score value. The color hue distinguishes domains while remaining redundant with x-position, supporting color-blind readers and casual scanning.

segments.label: 08 / 13 · THE DESIGN DECISIONS IN THE PA

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html: Adolphe Quetelet→Run this:→"Adolphe Quetelet"→Now make the prompt better.

beats.text: The ideas in this chapter didn't appear from nowhere. Adolphe Quetelet coined "the average man" in 1835 , proposing that human traits (height, chest circumference, criminal tendency) follow normal distributions. He gave statistics the conceptual tools to think about populations as distributions, not lists. Adolphe Quetelet, circa 1860. AI-generated portrait based on a public domain photograph (Wikimedia Commons).
